Glushon credited a former mentor (former longtime agent and current Detroit Pistons vice chairman Arn Tellem). He had extensive experience at Wasserman as an intern (2006-2009) and as an agent (2009-16). He praised his company’s head of sales and marketing (David Braun) and agent (Ryan Leff) for ensuring enough bandwidth to support their clients and families. But Holi

item.title: Ja Morant heads to Portland for Jerami Grant, Kris Murray
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading two-time NBA All-Star Ja Morant to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 seasons, hasn't played in 7 straight games for the Grizzlies since the 2022-23 season.

item.title: Contract details for Memphis/Portland
item.description: Bobby Marks: Contract details for Memphis/Portland To Portland Ja Morant 2026/27: $42.2M 2027/28: $44.9M To Memphis Jerami Grant 2026/27: $34.2M 2027/28: $37.2M (P) Kris Murray 2026/27: $5.3M

item.title: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be voided if the trade is executed today or tomorrow. If done on July 6, he would be entitled to a $13 million trade bonus. It would increase his $42.2 million salary to $48.7 million. Jerami Grant ($34.2 million) and Kris Murray ($5.3 million) matches for Morant on that $48.7 million salary. We'll see if he gets the entire amount or waives it.

item.title: Shams: Blazers not involved in Jaylen Brown talks, view acquiring Ja Morant as big move
item.description: SM Highlights: The Trail Blazers Aren’t Involved In Any Jaylen Brown Discussions. Per, @ShamsCharania “This was their big move as of right now my understanding is that they are not involved with Jaylen Brown, and they made attempts for Giannis, didn’t fall out.”

item.title: As a result of the trade happening today, he will not …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Ja Morant trade is official. As a result of the trade happening today, he will not receive his trade bonus. He carries a $42.2 million cap hit and the Trail Blazers maintain more cap flexibility. They are $27.6 million under the luxury tax line with 11 players rostered.

item.title: Ja Morant excited to be in Portland
item.description: According to Shams Charania of ESPN, the embattled star guard is reportedly thrilled to welcome new beginnings in Portland. "They felt like they had to take a swing just based on the talent that Ja Morant is and hoping that he has a chip on his shoulder, motivation to turn his career around," the NBA insider said about the Blazers trading for Morant. "The Blazers did speak to Ja Morant today, shortly after the trade. I'm told he expressed to them that he's excited to be in Portland."

item.title: Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard …
item.description: ESPN's Shams Charania reported Portland acquired Ja Morant via trade from the Memphis Grizzlies in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. According to NBA insider Chris Haynes, Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard in the backcourt alongside Deni Avdija on the wing following "a very unconventional move that the franchise believes has upside."

item.title: One executive from another team I talked to called the …
item.description: The Ja Morant trade caught many around the NBA by surprise, as the oft-injured Memphis Grizzlies guard was traded to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. One executive from another team I talked to called the deal “confusing," citing the Blazers' glut of guards with Damian Lillard returning from Achilles surgery and Jrue Holiday and Scoot Henderson under contract. He wondered whether there was a corresponding deal the Blazers were working on to clear up the position.

item.title: The Grizzlies saved a $13 million check to Morant by …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant trade, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the cap (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent. That matters because Morant has a 15 percent trade kicker on his deal, which doesn't matter between now and June 30 since his 2025-26 salary is already above the six-year-of-ser

item.title: What he saw, he said, disgusted him. Not the flagrant …
item.description: Five weeks ago I sat across from Dundon in North Carolina and he told me that he had watched “Untold: Jail Blazers,” the Netflix documentary on the era that has defined modern Trail Blazers history on a flight from Dallas to Raleigh. What he saw, he said, disgusted him. Not the flagrant and unapologetic drug use, the rape allegation or the bad attitudes, but the way fans turned on their team. His takeaway: The players didn’t do anything. Indeed, they were not caught on film waving around a handg

item.title: “Hopefully,” Dundon told me, “we’ll create a product …
item.description: When I had that conversation with Dundon in Raleigh, he said that he felt fans of the Portland teams of the early 2000s were too quick to latch onto negativity toward Blazers players. Damon’s weed. Rasheed’s tantrums. Bonzi’s birdie finger. Fans resented it all. “Hopefully,” Dundon told me, “we’ll create a product where that’s not a choice.” Indeed, there should be no choice at all for Blazers fans. The only option is unrestrained fury.

item.title: Blazers not expected to keep Robert Williams
item.description: The Blazers have immediate needs at center and power forward. They are not expected to retain free agent center Robert Williams III, who is hoping to land a contract in the $15 million a year range, leaving only second-year center Yang Hansen as a backup to Donovan Clingan.

item.title: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the …
item.description: Jake Fischer: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the Trail Blazers, but the Memphis Grizzlies did send $1 million in cash to Portland, sources say, in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray.

item.title: That is because Morant is still in year seven of his …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant deal, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the max (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent.

item.title: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be …
item.description: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be involved in trade discussions for the Celtics' Jaylen Brown, and that could be the next big move. Has Morant hit rock bottom? One high-level exec told ESPN over the weekend that he believes Morant’s needs extend beyond basketball. He actually believes, however, that Morant can make a comeback to his old form because of how the guard competes when he’s healthy.

item.title: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right …
item.description: Here's what ESPN's Shams Charania had to say on "SportsCenter" when asked whether the Portland Trail Blazers could make a move for the Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown after they traded for Ja Morant on Monday: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right now, my understanding is they're not involved with Jaylen Brown."

item.title: The Blazers now have four point guards — Morant, …
item.description: That leads to the unspoken part of this trade: more deals are on the way. Have to be. The Blazers now have four point guards — Morant, Lillard, Jrue Holiday and Henderson — so unloading one or two seems likely. If I was to guess, I would imagine Holiday, because of his contract and because of his skill set, is the more likely candidate to be traded next.

item.title: They want more big-man depth and realize they have a …
item.description: The Blazers are still in the market to make another deal. They want more big-man depth and realize they have a logjam of four point guards with Morant, Lillard, Jrue Holiday and Scoot Henderson, but the team does not feel it is essential to unload one of its guards. The Blazers believe the four guards can co-exist, in large part because the team believes Holiday can alleviate the redundancy by playing him off the ball as a shooting guard or small forward.

item.title: Donovan Clingan off limits in trade talks
item.description: One thing has become certain about the Trail Blazers after Monday’s trade for point guard Ja Morant: Portland center Donovan Clingan is now essentially off limits in trade talks, according to a team source.

item.title: The Blazers have interest in retaining all three …
item.description: The Blazers’ 15-man regular season roster features 10 players under guaranteed contract and two more — Vit Krejčí and Sidy Cissoko — with team options. The Blazers are expected to exercise both team options, giving them 12 players under contract. This leaves them with three unrestricted free agents: Robert Williams III, Matisse Thybulle and Blake Wesley. A team source told The Oregonian/OregonLive earlier this month the Blazers have interest in retaining all three players “if the deal makes se

item.title: They are not expected to retain free agent center …
item.description: The Blazers have immediate needs at center and power forward. They are not expected to retain free agent center Robert Williams III, who is hoping to land a contract in the $15 million a year range, leaving only second-year center Yang Hansen as a backup to Donovan Clingan. The Blazers are also thin at power forward, with Deni Avdija and Toumani Camara their only options.

item.title: Blazers, Robert Williams III agree to three-year, $44 million contract extension
item.description: Shams Charania: Portland Trail Blazers center Robert Williams III has agreed to sign a three-year, $44 million contract extension to return to the Portland Trail Blazers through 2028-29, agent Kevin Bradbury of LIFT Sports Management tells ESPN.

item.title: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown …
item.description: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown for some time now, but all indications are that their interest is moderate, at best. And, again, nothing close to what the Celtics have been asking for, according to sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: Kelly Oubre expected to meet with Sixers, Pacers, Blazers, Lakers, others
item.description: Kelly Iko: Free agent wing Kelly Oubre Jr. plans to meet with the 76ers, Pacers, Blazers and Lakers among others, sources tell @YahooSports.

item.title: Branden Carlson signs with Blazers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent 7-footer Branden Carlson has agreed to a one-year, $2.5 million deal to sign with the Portland Trail Blazers, sources tell ESPN. Portland picks up a big man who has developed in the OKC Thunder system over the last two seasons, finalizing a deal with @PrioritySports ' Ross Aroyo and Mark Bartelstein.

item.title: I LOVE ROBERT WILLIAMS
item.description: Toumani Buckets: I LOVE ROBERT WILLIAMS

item.title: Robert Williams' extension is only fully guaranteed in first year
item.description: Jake Fischer: Dean Wade's deal with the Sixers has a partial guarantee in Year 4, per source. In Portland, I'm told Robert Williams' extension is fully guaranteed at $14 million for this season, but just $5 million guaranteed in Year 2, which becomes fully guaranteed if he plays 50 games this 2026-27 campaign. Then, Year 3 is entirely non-guaranteed, but becomes fully guaranteed if Williams plays 50 games in 27-28. Wonky...

item.title: Portland Trail Blazers signed Robert Williams to a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Portland Trail Blazers signed Robert Williams to a 3-year, $44 million deal, which includes $5 million partially guaranteed Year 2, and is fully non-guaranteed Year 3. Years 2 and 3 become fully guaranteed if he plays 50+ regular-season games in the prior season.

item.title: Blazers head coach Micah Nori: I think we are going to get the best version of Ja Morant
item.description: For two hours, they dined and talked in a South Carolina steakhouse, and what Portland Trail Blazers coach Micah Nori noticed most about his lunch partner was the sincerity of his words, the seriousness of his facial expressions, and the excitement that was unable to be contained. Ja Morant told his new coach that he couldn’t wait to play for the Blazers. “I think we are going to get the best version of Ja,” Nori said. “I think we are going to get a motivated Ja.”

item.title: “The way he was talking, the way he looked … the …
item.description: When Nori settled into the steakhouse booth on Tuesday, he saw none of the petulance, none of the defiance, none of the attitude that has colored Morant’s image. Nori said he couldn’t help but notice the way Morant talked, and the way he carried himself. There was a seriousness, an enthusiasm, a focus. “The way he was talking, the way he looked … the intent he had, you could just tell in his face that he missed basketball,” Nori said. “And he assured that we were going to get the best Ja and tha

item.title: “He’s excited to be on a team with vets like Dame and …
item.description: “He’s excited to be on a team with vets like Dame and Jrue, and he loves the young talent of Deni (Avdija), Scoot, (Donovan) Clingan, (Toumani) Camara … I mean, he mentioned them all by name,” Nori said. As they left the restaurant, Nori asked Morant what he needed from him. How could he help? “He just said, ‘Make sure I get some Trail Blazers gear as soon as possible so I can start wearing it,’” Nori said.

item.title: The Blazers have announced the signing of Branden …
item.description: Sean Highkin: The Blazers have announced the signing of Branden Carlson. Because it's a one-year minimum contract, it can be made official before the moratorium period ends on July 6.

item.title: Grizzlies engaged with Bucks on Ja Morant up until Monday
item.description: The Milwaukee Bucks, league sources told The Athletic, engaged the Grizzlies on potential trades for Morant as early as January, when it became known Morant was on the market. Those sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe the deliberations, said discussions continued periodically up until Monday, when the Bucks chose not to go through with the trade.

item.title: The Blazers have announced their Summer League roster, …
item.description: Sean Highkin: The Blazers have announced their Summer League roster, which features Yang Hansen and the two two-ways they currently have under contract, Jayson Kent and Chris Youngblood. Gil Abraham, one of their player development coaches, will be head coach.

item.title: No NBA team has fewer summer league players than Tom Dundon's Trail Blazers
item.description: Jorge Sierra: As of now, the Blazers' summer league roster features only 12 players, the fewest of any NBA team that has announced its roster so far. No other team has fewer than 15. Very on brand!

item.title: Ja Morant will wear No. 1 with the Trail Blazers next …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Ja Morant will wear No. 1 with the Trail Blazers next season, the team announced 🔥 The new number replaces the No. 12 Morant has worn at every level since high school.

item.title: I think that's what they're looking to build off of, …
item.description: "Obviously I didn't know it was going to happen," Boozer said July 2. "Ja is a great player. It's not our decision to make. It's the front office's (decision). I think we got a great young core. I think that's what they're looking to build off of, so I think (the future) is really exciting. "Obviously it sucks to lose a guy as good as Ja, what he's done for the city and the team, but it's part of the business."

item.title: 76ers agree to sign Rayan Rupert to a two-way contract
item.description: Shams Charania: The Philadelphia 76ers have agreed to sign French guard Rayan Rupert on a two-way NBA deal, sources tell ESPN. Rupert, a second-round pick in 2023, split last season in Memphis and Portland.

item.title: Blazers' analytics rate Jaylen Brown as a 'negative player'
item.description: Another team with widely reported interest, the Trail Blazers, similarly did not join the chase. A Blazers team source said the Blazers were never enamored with Brown, even before Boston made it known he was available on the trade market. Two factors went into the Blazers’ disinterest: Their analytics viewed him as a negative player and the Celtics’ asking price was too high. “We were never aggressively looking to trade for him,” a team source said. “And particularly not at their price.”

item.title: Caleb Love to 76ers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Caleb Love has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Philadelphia 76ers, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: “He’s awesome. Excellent communicator and great human …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Blazers Summer League head coach Gil Abraham on his early impressions of working with Micah Nori: “He’s awesome. Excellent communicator and great human being. But he’s got an edge, too, which is needed. I think guys are really going to enjoy playing for him.”

item.title: Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized …
item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

item.title: Jay Triano joins Micah Nori's coaching stuff in Portland
item.description: Chris Haynes: Longtime NBA offensive guru Jay Triano has agreed to join the coaching staff of Portland Trail Blazers head coach Micah Nori and reunite with franchise star Damian Lillard, league sources say

item.title: Micah Nori was an assistant under Jay Triano in …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Micah Nori was an assistant under Jay Triano in Toronto nearly 2 decades ago. Very cool reunion.

item.title: Blazers claim Micah Potter off waivers
item.description: The Trail Blazers have claimed former Pacers big man Micah Potter off the waiver wire, according to the NBA’s official transaction log.

item.title: The Blazers have claimed Micah Potter off waivers from …
item.description: Sean Highkin: The Blazers have claimed Micah Potter off waivers from Indiana, the team announced.

item.title: Micah Nori on Chris Finch: 'There's no paranoia with him'
item.description: "How much of his style helped you prepare for what you are about to embark upon?" Micah Nori: "The one thing that coach Finch is, he is very very good at what he does. Number one, there's no paranoia with him and the other thing is he will let you coach and gives you a voice. When those things happen, I was fortunate to be with him for a long time and work with him. Philosophy-wise and the way we think about basketball is very very similar."

item.title: “You’ll see."
item.description: Drew Hill: I asked Ja Morant if this trade will add to the chip on his shoulder. “Oh, for sure,” he said. In what ways? “You’ll see."

item.title: "I'm definitely grateful for my time in Memphis," he …
item.description: Drew Hill: Ja Morant is welcoming a fresh start. He said he wants to show Portland a "different Ja." But that reality is still sinking in. "I'm definitely grateful for my time in Memphis," he said. "That will always be home for me."

item.title: “I’ve done what I’ve done in the past, but it’s been …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Blazers guard Ja Morant said his “bad guy” image is a misconception: “I’ve done what I’ve done in the past, but it’s been addressed and handled already. I don’t see why years later that’s still the topic when nothing’s happened since.”

item.title: Ja Morant still adjusting to life with the Portland …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Ja Morant still adjusting to life with the Portland Trail Blazers after the trade from the Memphis Grizzlies. “Oh my God, I signed with the number 12!”
